protocol: Run round-table Phases 0-2 ONLY (no spawns), k=5 per case. Score: (1) TIER accuracy where expected_tier is set (auto|offer|silent) — the NAG-RATE (offers on expected_tier=silent cases) must be 0; (2) persona-set precision/recall against expected_personas for size 3-5 cases; exact-class accuracy for size 0/1 cases; (3) where expected_red_team is set, exact-match the assignment (never product-strategist, never twice). Cases without expected_tier are legacy sizing cases: treat them as post-consent (tier already passed). Tune description and references/routing.md on split=train; report final numbers on split=val only. Report pass^k, not best-of-k. Retired aliases appearing in any pick = automatic case failure.

cases.request: Plan the migration of our auth service to the new identity provider.
What's the best way to approach adding multi-tenancy to the platform?
We're thinking of moving our internal services from REST to gRPC.
gRPC looks cool, might be fun to try it on a side project someday.
Add a retry to this function and put a short plan comment above it.
Get me multiple expert takes on this caching design.
Drop the legacy_orders table today.
What's our team's on-call schedule plan for next month?
Draft our Q4 engineering roadmap.
Is it worth switching our CI from Jenkins to GitHub Actions before we commit the platform budget?
Where is the migration plan document for the v2 rollout?
cases.note: near-miss: contains 'migration plan' but is a file lookup, not a decision

cases.request: What's the deployment plan we agreed on last sprint?
cases.note: near-miss: 'plan' but recalling a decided fact, not making one

cases.request: Rename getUser to fetchUser across the repo.
Add a docstring to this function.
What database are we using in staging?
Explain how our OAuth refresh flow works.
Fix the lint errors in this file.
Kubernetes seems interesting, might play with it sometime.
cases.note: near-miss: tech musing, no decision asked

cases.request: Run the test suite and tell me what fails.
Summarize what this PR changes.
Is this regex correct for matching emails?
Bump the lodash dependency to the latest patch version.
cases.note: near-miss: a dependency change, but routine patch bump, not codebase-wide adoption

cases.request: Commit these changes with a sensible message.
What's the difference between a mutex and a semaphore?
Add pagination to the users list endpoint the same way the orders endpoint does it.
cases.note: near-miss: routine implementation with an obvious existing pattern, no trade-off

cases.request: Should we store timestamps as UTC or local time in the new events table?
cases.note: offer among near-misses: a real schema decision, must NOT be silenced

cases.request: Pressure-test our disaster-recovery plan.
cases.note: auto among near-misses: explicit scrutiny request

cases.request: Should we split the billing module out of the monolith into its own service?
Pressure-test this launch plan for the new onboarding flow before we commit the quarter to it.
What does HTTP status 429 mean?
Just give me a security review of this auth-token change.
We're torn between Postgres JSONB and a dedicated events table for the activity feed — which and why?
Rename this variable and fix the typo in the README.
Should we adopt this new ORM across the whole codebase?
Compare these two HTTP client libraries' APIs for our use case.
We're changing the events schema that three consumers read — how should we roll it out?
Fix the failing test in tests/test_auth.py.
Is this cron-based cleanup job safe to run in production every night?
Build vs buy: should we integrate a vendor feature-flag service or keep our homegrown flags?
What's the difference between optimistic and pessimistic locking?
We want to rewrite the frontend in a new framework next quarter — sanity-check this before we pitch it.
Does this function handle unicode filenames correctly?
From a pure data-integrity standpoint, is soft-delete or hard-delete right for our audit tables?
Migrate this table to the new schema.
We can ship the quick fix now or the real fix in two weeks — the quick fix adds a config flag users will see. Which do we do?
What are the pros and cons of using UUIDs as primary keys?
Should our public API move from offset pagination to cursor pagination, given the mobile clients we can't force-upgrade?
Should our signup flow start collecting date-of-birth for personalization?
We want to redesign the CLI's flag names for v2 — worth the breaking change?
Is it OK to log the full request body for debugging in production?
From a testing standpoint only, can we safely delete the legacy checkout path?
Should we add a third-party analytics SDK to the mobile app?
How does our OAuth refresh flow work?
Our error messages are cryptic — should we invest a sprint in rewriting them?
Should we switch CI providers? Builds are slow but migration is a quarter of platform work.
Add retry logic to the webhook sender.
We're considering storing user sessions in a signed cookie instead of Redis — good idea?
Is our test pyramid wrong? E2E takes 40 minutes and unit coverage is thin.
Should we expose an undocumented admin endpoint to a partner for a pilot?
What's our current p95 latency on the search endpoint?
GDPR question: can we keep deleted users' order history for analytics?
Should we deprecate the v1 API this year? Some enterprise customers still call it daily.
